bitty documentation

Documentation

data-* attributes

Overview

  • The HTML side of the bitty API consists of these four attributes.
  • Attributes can be added to HTML elements to send and receive signals through JavaScript methods.

Items

data-connect
Description
  • `data-connect` is an optional attribute that can be added to bitty-8-0 tags.
  • If a `data-connect` attribute exists bitty will attempt to load the module defined by the attribute's value.
  • If a bitty-8-0 tag doesn't have a `data-connect` attribute it will attempt to load a module class from `window.BittyClass` as a fallback.
Examples
Load a default class from `window.BittyClass` if no `data-connect` attribute exists on a `bitty-8-0` tag. [0010-default-bitty-class-on-window]
HTML
<bitty-8-0></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_DA553">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.BittyClass = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("signal_DA553");
  }

  signal_DA553(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Load a `window.NamedClass` directly from that page when the value of `data-connect` is `NamedClass` (and not a remote path). [0020-connect-to-class-on-page]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5EF10"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_5EF10">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5EF10 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("signal_5EF10");
  }

  signal_5EF10(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Connect to the default class from a remote module. [0030-connection-to-default-module-class]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/includes/0010-data-attributes/items/data-connect/tests/0030-connection-to-default-module-class/remote-javascript.js"
></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_15881">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
// this content is in an external file
// TODO: show the external file here.
</script>
Results
bug
Connect to a specific class from a remote module. [0040-connect-to-specific-remote-class]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/includes/0010-data-attributes/items/data-connect/tests/0040-connect-to-specific-remote-class/remote-javascript.js:ExampleClass"
></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CD4FD">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
// this content is in an external file
// TODO: show the external file here.
</script>
Results
bug
data-listeners
Description
Examples
Listen for a specific event type. [0010-listen-for-event]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9CE5F"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="el-9CE5F" 
  data-listeners="bittylocallistenertest"
  data-send="signal_9CE5F"
></div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_9CE5F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9CE5F = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-9CE5F");
    const testEvent = new TestEventClass9CE5F();
    testSender.dispatchEvent(testEvent);
  }

  signal_9CE5F(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};

// This test uses a custom event instead of
// something like mouseover to facilitate
// automated test output.
class TestEventClass9CE5F extends Event {
  constructor() {
    super("bittylocallistenertest", { bubbles: true });
  }
}
</script>
Results
bug
Only send signals when the specified listener is fired (i.e. don't respond to `click` or `input` unless they are included in the `data-listeners` value). [0020-only-send-for-specified-events]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC23DC"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="el-C23DC" 
  data-listeners="miscellaneousevent"
  data-send="signal_C23DC"
></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C23DC">ok</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC23DC = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-C23DC").click();
  }

  signal_C23DC(_, el) {
    // set to `bug` here since this
    // signal should not fire.
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
};
</script>
Results
ok
Set listeners globally for a bitty class directly from the bitty-8-0 tag. [0030-set-listeners-globally]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-listeners="bittygloballistenertest"
  data-connect="ClassAC896"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="el-AC896" data-send="signal_AC896"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_AC896">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassAC896 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-AC896");
    const testEvent = new TestEventClassAC896();
    testSender.dispatchEvent(testEvent);
  }

  signal_AC896(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};

// This test uses a custom event instead of
// something like mouseover to facilitate
// automated test output.
class TestEventClassAC896 extends Event {
  constructor() {
    super("bittygloballistenertest", { bubbles: true });
  }
}
</script>
Results
bug
Setting a global `data-listeners` attribute on a `bitty-8-0` tag removes the default `click` and `input` listeners. [0035-global-listener-removes-defaults]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-listeners="miscellaneousgloballistenertest"
  data-connect="ClassE7E76"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="el-E7E76" data-send="signal_E7E76"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E7E76">ok</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE7E76 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-E7E76").click();
  }

  signal_E7E76(_, el) {
    // set to `bug` here since this
    // signal should not fire.
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
};
</script>
Results
ok
The default `click` and `input` listeners can be added back in to the `data-listeners` attribute on a `bitty-8-0` element. [0037-add-default-to-globals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-listeners="miscellaneousgloballistenertest click input"
  data-connect="Class22223"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="el-22223" data-send="signal_22223"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_22223">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class22223 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-22223").click();
  }

  signal_22223(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Setting an empty string for the value of data-listener on an element prevents it from sending signals. [0040-empty-string-turns-off-listeners]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1AE48"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="el-1AE48" 
  data-listeners=""
  data-send="signal_1AE48"
></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_1AE48">ok</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class1AE48 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-1AE48").click();
  }

  signal_1AE48(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
};
</script>
Results
ok
The default `click` and `input` events can be added back in alongside specified listeners. [0050-include-default-with-custom]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class4BCE1"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="el-4BCE1" 
  data-listeners="mouseover bittytest click input"
  data-send="signal_4BCE1"
></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_4BCE1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class4BCE1 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-4BCE1").click();
  }

  signal_4BCE1(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Notes
  • The specific set of custom listeners is defined based on elements that exist with a `data-listeners` attribute when the module loads. That is, if a new element is added after a bitty module has initialized with a new listener type in a `data-listener` attribute value it won't be picked up.
data-receive
Description
Examples
Receive a single signal. [0010-single-data-receive]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class90DD9"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_90DD9">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class90DD9 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("signal_90DD9");
  }

  signal_90DD9(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Receive multiple signals. [0020-receive-multiple-signals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCBE64"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="test" 
  data-receive="signal_CBE64 signal_CBE64_2 signal_CBE64_3"
>bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCBE64 = class {
  #updates = [];

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger(
      `signal_CBE64 signal_CBE64_2 signal_CBE64_3`,
    );
  }

  signal_CBE64(_, el) {
    this.#updates.push("bug");
    el.innerHTML = this.#updates[0];
  }

  signal_CBE64_2(_, el) {
    this.#updates.push("bug");
    el.innerHTML = this.#updates[1];
  }

  signal_CBE64_3(_, el) {
    if (this.#updates.length === 2) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Multiple elements receive the same signal. [0030-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class327E5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class327E5 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger(`signal_327E5`);
  }

  signal_327E5(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
data-send
Description
Examples
Send a single signal from a click event. [0010-basic-send]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassABF58"></bitty-8-0>
<button id="el-ABF58" data-send="signal_ABF58">
  Test Button
</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ABF58">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassABF58 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-ABF58").click();
  }

  signal_ABF58(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Send a single signal that's picked up by multiple receivers. [0020-single-to-multiple]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class16851"></bitty-8-0>
<button id="el-16851" data-send="signal_16851">
  Test Button
</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_16851">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_16851">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_16851">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class16851 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-16851").click();
  }

  signal_16851(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Send multiple signals at the same time. [0030-send-multiple-signals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class148C2"></bitty-8-0>
<button 
  id="el-148C2" 
  data-send="signal_148C2 signal_148C2_2 signal_148C2_3"
>Test Button</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_148C2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_148C2_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_148C2_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class148C2 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-148C2").click();
  }

  signal_148C2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }

  signal_148C2_2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }

  signal_148C2_3(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Signals are processed synchronously and in order by default. [0040-sync-default]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class136FB"></bitty-8-0>
<button 
  id="el-136FB" 
  data-send="signal_136FB signal_136FB_2 signal_136FB_3"
>Test Button</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_136FB">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_136FB_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_136FB_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class136FB = class {
  #count = 0;

  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-136FB").click();
  }

  signal_136FB(_, el) {
    this.#count += 1;
    if (this.#count === 1) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  signal_136FB_2(_, el) {
    this.#count += 1;
    if (this.#count === 2) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  signal_136FB_3(_, el) {
    this.#count += 1;
    if (this.#count === 3) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Async methods can be awaited. [0050-async-await]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7C304"></bitty-8-0>
<button 
  id="el-7C304" 
  data-send="await:signal_7C304 signal_7C304_2"
>Test Button</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_7C304_2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7C304 = class {
  #time1 = performance.now();

  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-7C304").click();
  }

  async signal_7C304(_, el) {
    await this.sleep(50);
  }

  signal_7C304_2(_, el) {
    const time2 = performance.now();
    if ((time2 - this.#time1) > 45) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Signals can be sent without corresponding `data-receive="SIGNAL"` elements. When that happens, the fire only once. [0060-no-receive-required]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class0773E"></bitty-8-0>
<button 
  id="el-0773E" 
  data-send="signal_0773E"
>Test Button</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_0773E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class0773E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-0773E").click();
  }

  signal_0773E(_, __) {
    this.trigger("verify_signal_0773E");
  }

  verify_signal_0773E(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug

bit Methods and Properties

Overview

The different types of content are:

  • [x] Element: [x] create - [x] delete - [x] fetch - [x] load - [x] render - [x] update

  • [x] Fragment: [x] create - [x] delete - [x] fetch - [x] load - [x] render - [x] update

  • [] SVG: [x] create - [x] delete - [x] fetch - [x] load - [ ] render - [ ] update

  • [] JSON: [ ] create - [ ] delete - [ ] fetch - [ ] load - [ ] save

Items

addJSON(key, data) [Done]
Description
  • Adds a new entry with `key` to `this.json[key]`.
Examples
Create a new `this.json[key]` entry from a string. [0010-add-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class59B7E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_59B7E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class59B7E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_59B7E");
  }

  given_signal_59B7E(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_59B7E");
  }

  test_signal_59B7E(_, el) {
    const jsonString = `{ "status": "ok" }`;
    this.addJSON("data_signal_59B7E", jsonString);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_59B7E"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Create a new `this.json[key]` entry from a JSON object. [0020-json-object-add]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA55AA"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A55AA">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA55AA = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A55AA");
  }

  given_signal_A55AA(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_A55AA");
  }

  test_signal_A55AA(_, el) {
    const jsonObject = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
    this.addJSON("data_signal_A55AA", jsonObject);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_A55AA"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Create a new `this.json[key]` entry from a JavaScript Object. [0022-objects-added]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class51037"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_51037">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class51037 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_51037");
  }

  given_signal_51037(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_51037");
  }

  test_signal_51037(_, el) {
    const jsObject = { status: "ok" };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_51037", jsObject);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_51037"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return result `ok` as `true` when a string is added. [0025-strings-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF4BCA"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_F4BCA">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF4BCA = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_F4BCA");
  }

  given_signal_F4BCA(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_F4BCA");
  }

  test_signal_F4BCA(_, el) {
    const jsonString = `{}`;
    const result = this.addJSON("data_signal_F4BCA", jsonString);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return result `ok` as `true` when objects are added. [0027-objects-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF9161"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_F9161">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF9161 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_F9161");
  }

  given_signal_F9161(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_F9161");
  }

  test_signal_F9161(_, el) {
    const jsonObject = JSON.parse(`{}`);
    const result = this.addJSON("data_signal_F9161", jsonObject);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return result `ok` as `false` if a string is not valid JSON. [0030-returns-error]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA76D8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A76D8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA76D8 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A76D8");
  }

  given_signal_A76D8(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_A76D8");
  }

  test_signal_A76D8(_, el) {
    const invalidJSON = "this is not valid JSON";
    const result = this.addJSON("data_signal_A76D8", invalidJSON);
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
New entries from strings are added to localStorage under a `data` key. [0040-string-to-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC117E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_C117E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC117E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_C117E");
  }

  given_signal_C117E(_, __) {
    localStorage.removeItem("bittyJSON_data_signal_C117E");
    const jsonString = `{ "status": "ok" }`;
    this.addJSON("data_signal_C117E", jsonString);
    this.trigger("test_signal_C117E");
  }

  test_signal_C117E(_, el) {
    const verify =
      JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("bittyJSON_data_signal_C117E")).data;
    el.innerHTML = verify.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
New entries from objects are added to localStorage under a `data` key. [0045-object-to-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA4EF0"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A4EF0">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA4EF0 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A4EF0");
  }

  given_signal_A4EF0(_, __) {
    localStorage.removeItem("bittyJSON_data_signal_A4EF0");
    const jsonObject = { status: "ok" };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_A4EF0", jsonObject);
    this.trigger("test_signal_A4EF0");
  }

  test_signal_A4EF0(_, el) {
    const verify =
      JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("bittyJSON_data_signal_A4EF0")).data;
    el.innerHTML = verify.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
JavaScript objects are turned into JSON objects which removes any methods they have. [0050-objects-stringified]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassD35C5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_D35C5">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassD35C5 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_D35C5");
  }

  given_signal_D35C5(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_D35C5");
  }

  test_signal_D35C5(_, el) {
    const jsObject = { method_D35C5: () => {} };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_D35C5", jsObject);
    const verify = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("data_signal_D35C5")).data;
    if (verify.method_D35C5 === undefined) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Adding a JSON with an existing `key` overwrites the earlier version. [0080-overwrite-existing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class12A9E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_12A9E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class12A9E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_12A9E");
  }

  given_signal_12A9E(_, __) {
    const initialObject = { status: "failed" };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_12A9E", initialObject);
    this.trigger("test_signal_12A9E");
  }

  test_signal_12A9E(_, el) {
    const overwritingObject = { status: "ok" };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_12A9E", overwritingObject);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_12A9E"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an error if no argument for the JSON was passed in. [0090-error-on-nothing-passed-in]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE60E9"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E60E9">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE60E9 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E60E9");
  }

  given_signal_E60E9(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_E60E9");
  }

  test_signal_E60E9(_, el) {
    const result = this.addJSON("data_signal_E60E9");
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Notes
  • Items stored in localStorage are moved under a top level `data` key in the object and stringified. (e.g. `{ "example": "alfa" }` becomes the string `{ "data": { "example": "alfa" } }` in localStorage.
  • The related bit methods work with the same top level `data` key in local storage. Those methods being:
    • `.fetchJSON(url [, options = {}])`
    • `.loadJSON(key, fallback)`
    • `.removeJSON(key)`
    • `.saveJSON(key)`
createElement(key, content) [Done]
Description
  • Adds a element to the `this.element[key]` collection
Examples
Add from a string. [0010-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class2B6BB"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_2B6BB">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class2B6BB = class {
  #key = "el_signal_2B6BB";

  test_signal_2B6BB(_, el) {
    const elementString = `<div class="test">ok</div>`;
    this.createElement(this.#key, elementString);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_2B6BB");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Creating an element returns a result object with an `ok` value of true if the element was added successfully. [0015-ok-true]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassEE7C0"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_EE7C0">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassEE7C0 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_EE7C0";

  test_signal_EE7C0(_, el) {
    const elementString = `<div>ok</div>`;
    const result = this.createElement(this.#key, elementString);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_EE7C0");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Creating an element returns a result object with an `ok` value of `false` if the element could not be added. [0018-ok-false]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class2C3F3"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_2C3F3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class2C3F3 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_2C3F3";

  test_signal_2C3F3(_, el) {
    const result = this.createElement(this.#key, {
      misc: "invalid payload",
    });
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_2C3F3");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use an existing element. [0020-from-element]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC54A9"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_C54A9">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC54A9 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_C54A9";

  test_signal_C54A9(_, el) {
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.classList.add("test");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.createElement(this.#key, newEl);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_C54A9");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use a document fragment (in which case the first child of the fragment is used. Any other elements are ignored.) [0030-from-fragment]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassDA138"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_DA138">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassDA138 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_DA138";

  test_signal_DA138(_, el) {
    const template = document.createElement("template");
    template.innerHTML = `<div class="test">ok</div>`;
    this.createElement(this.#key, template.content);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_DA138");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
If there is more than one element in a document fragment only the first one is used. A warning is logged and returned. [0035-warn-on-extra-fragment-elements]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class4C991"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_4C991">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class4C991 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_4C991";

  test_signal_4C991(_, el) {
    const template = document.createElement("template");
    template.innerHTML = `<div></div><div></div>`;
    const result = this.createElement(this.#key, template.content);
    if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_4C991");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Elements are added to storage automatically. [0040-store-elements]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class6CF16"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_6CF16">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class6CF16 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_6CF16";

  test_signal_6CF16(_, el) {
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
    delete this._element[this.#key];
    this.loadElement(this.#key);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_6CF16");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Adding an element with the same key as an existing item overwrites the older one. The returned result is a `warn` with a message about the overwrite. The `result.ok` value is still `true`. [0050-new-overwrite-old]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class374D9"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_374D9">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class374D9 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_374D9";

  test_signal_374D9(_, el) {
    const result = this.createElement(this.#key, "<div>ok</div>");
    if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, "<div>bug</div>");
    this.trigger("test_signal_374D9");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an error if no value was passed in with which to make the element. [0060-error-if-no-value]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class73040"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_73040">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class73040 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_73040";

  test_signal_73040(_, el) {
    const result = this.createElement(this.#key);
    if (result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_73040");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an error result if something other than a String, Element, or Document Fragment was passed to the method. [0070-error-invalid-input]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC244B"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_C244B">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC244B = class {
  #key = "el_signal_C244B";

  test_signal_C244B(_, el) {
    const result = this.createElement(this.#key, { misc: "not a valid input" });
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_C244B");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Notes
  • TODO: Display parsing HTML Footnote
createFragment(key, content) [Done]
Description
Examples
Add from a string [0010-add-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class2C4FE"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_2C4FE">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class2C4FE = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_2C4FE";

  test_signal_2C4FE(_, el) {
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div>x</div><div>ok</div>`);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_2C4FE");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and provide success result. [0020-ok-true-on-success]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassD2EBD"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_D2EBD">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassD2EBD = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_D2EBD";

  test_signal_D2EBD(_, el) {
    const result = this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div>x</div><div>ok</div>`);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_D2EBD");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Error on fail [0030-ok-false-on-failure]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC8DB8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_C8DB8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC8DB8 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_C8DB8";

  test_signal_C8DB8(_, el) {
    const result = this.createFragment(this.#key, {
      key: "not a string, element, or document fragment",
    });
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_C8DB8");
  }

  given_signal_C8DB8(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_C8DB8");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
From fragment. [0040-from-fragment]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA0EAB"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A0EAB">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA0EAB = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_A0EAB";

  test_signal_A0EAB(fragment, el) {
    this.createFragment(this.#key, fragment);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A0EAB");
  }

  given_signal_A0EAB(_, __) {
    const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const element = document.createElement("div");
    element.innerHTML = "ok";
    fragment.appendChild(element);
    this.send(fragment, "test_signal_A0EAB");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
From element. [0050-from-element]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class69369"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_69369">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class69369 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_69369";

  test_signal_69369(element, el) {
    this.createFragment(this.#key, element);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_69369");
  }

  given_signal_69369(_, __) {
    const element = document.createElement("div");
    element.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.send(element, "test_signal_69369");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Store automatically. [0060-store-automatically]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC87DB"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_C87DB">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC87DB = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_C87DB";

  test_signal_C87DB(element, el) {
    const result = this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_C87DB");
  }

  given_signal_C87DB(_, __) {
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    delete this._fragment[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_C87DB");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Warn if overwriting existing key. [0070-warn-on-overwrite]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class3D5CF"></bitty-8-0>

JavaScript

  test_signal_3D5CF(_, el) {
    const result = this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_3D5CF");
  }

  given_signal_3D5CF(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_3D5CF");
  }
};
</script>

  test_signal_FE03B(_, el) {

    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {

  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {

    this.trigger("test_signal_FE03B");

};

bug
createSVG(key, content) [Done]
Description
Examples
Add from a string [0010-add-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class58C94"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_58C94">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_58C94"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class58C94 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_58C94";

  test_signal_58C94(input, el) {
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_58C94");
  }

  view_signal_58C94(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.send(input, "test_signal_58C94");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
From another SVG. [0040-from-svg]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5112B"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_5112B">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_5112B"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5112B = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_5112B";

  test_signal_5112B(input, el) {
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_5112B");
  }

  view_signal_5112B(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const input = document.createElementNS("http://www.w3.org/2000/svg", "svg");
    input.setAttribute("width", "60");
    input.setAttribute("height", "40");
    input.innerHTML = `<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>`;
    this.send(input, "test_signal_5112B");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Store automatically. [0060-store-automatically]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassD4DA1"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_D4DA1">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_D4DA1"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassD4DA1 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_D4DA1";

  test_signal_D4DA1(_, el) {
    this.loadSVG(this.#key);
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_D4DA1");
  }

  view_signal_D4DA1(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeSVG(this.#key);
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    delete this._svg[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_D4DA1");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Warn if overwriting existing key. [0070-warn-on-overwrite]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassFACB7"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_FACB7">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassFACB7 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_FACB7";

  test_signal_FACB7(input, el) {
    const result = this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    this.send(input, "test_signal_FACB7");
  }
};
</script>

No key and no fallback returns error. [0090-no-key-no-value-returns-error]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class226E6"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_226E6">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class226E6 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_226E6";

  test_signal_226E6(_, el) {
    const result = this.createSVG();
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_226E6");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
deleteElement(key) [Done]
Description
Examples
Remove and existing element. [0010-remove-existing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE84D4"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E84D4">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE84D4 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_E84D4";

  test_signal_E84D4(_, el) {
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    if (this.renderElement(this.#key) === undefined) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_E84D4");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug

JavaScript

  test_signal_DE329(_, el) {
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    const result = this.loadElement(this.#key);
    if (result.level === "error" && result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_DE329");
  }
};
</script>

    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";

  }

  // Test Setup

    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_06635");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
deleteFragment(key) [Done]
Description
  • Deletes a fragment with the given key from the collection of fragments.
  • Deletes it from storage as well
Examples
Delete an existing fragment from the collection. [0010-remove-existing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class108CD"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_108CD">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class108CD = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_108CD";

  test_signal_108CD(_, el) {
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    if (this.fragment[this.#key] === undefined) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_108CD");
  }

  given_signal_108CD(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, "<div></div>");
    this.trigger("test_signal_108CD");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Fragments are deleted from storage as well. [0020-storage-removal]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE279E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E279E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>

  test_signal_E279E(_, el) {
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    const result = this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E279E");
  }

  given_signal_E279E(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, "<div></div>");
    this.trigger("test_signal_E279E");
  }
};
</script>

HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB0E93"></bitty-8-0>

JavaScript
<script>

Results
bug
deleteSVG(key)
Description
Removes an SVG from the collection and storage.
Examples
Remove and existing element. [0010-remove-existing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCB6CF"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_CB6CF">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCB6CF = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_CB6CF";

  test_signal_CB6CF(_, el) {
    this.deleteSVG(this.#key);
    if (this.renderSVG(this.#key) === undefined) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    this.trigger("test_signal_CB6CF");
  }
};
</script>
Results

Elements are removed from storage as well. [0020-storage-removal]

    this.deleteSVG(this.#key);
    const result = this.loadSVG(this.#key);

<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;

    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    this.trigger("test_signal_1D251");

};

bug
fetchElement(key, url, options = {}) [Done]
Description
  • Fetch HTML from a URL and store it as an element in `this.element[key]`.
Examples
Fetch an element from a URL. [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class49058"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_49058">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class49058 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_49058";

  async test_signal_49058(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    await this.fetchElement(this.#key, url);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_49058");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
`fetchElement()` has a return value object with `.ok === true` when the fetch succeeds. [0030-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class0938C"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_0938C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class0938C = class {
  #key = "el_signal_0938C";

  async test_signal_0938C(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    const result = await this.fetchElement(this.#key, url);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_0938C");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
If the incoming data is a Document Fragment only the first element is used. A warn message is logged and returned as the result. [0032-fragments-truncated]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE0510"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E0510">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE0510 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_E0510";

  async test_signal_E0510(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
    const result = await this.fetchElement(this.#key, url);
    if (
      result.level === "warn"
    ) {
      el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_E0510");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Fetched elements are added to storage. [0035-add-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class92749"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_92749">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class92749 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_92749";

  async test_signal_92749(_, el) {
    this.loadElement(this.#key);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  async bittyReady() {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    await this.fetchElement(this.#key, url);
    delete this._element[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_92749");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Return a result with `level === "error"` and `ok === false` if there's an issue with the fetch. [0040-fetch-issue]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassEFEFA"></bitty-8-0>
<p>NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in 
  the console. The test is run manually to prevent
  the noise from showing up on initial page load.</p>
<button data-send="signal_EFEFA">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_EFEFA">waiting</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassEFEFA = class {
  #key = "el_signal_EFEFA";

  async signal_EFEFA(_, el) {
    const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
    const result = await this.fetchElement(this.#key, url);
    if (result.level === "error" && result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in the console. The test is run manually to prevent the noise from showing up on initial page load.

waiting
If a fetch overwrites an existing element a warning is logged and returned with `level === "warn"` and `ok === true`. [0060-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class97CD8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_97CD8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class97CD8 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_97CD8";

  async test_signal_97CD8(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    const result = await this.fetchElement(this.#key, url);
    if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div>first</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_97CD8");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Pass options to a fetch call. [0080-pass-options]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7572B"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have
  the ability to modify responses based on 
  request options. Verification is done manually. 
  This automated test is set to always pass
  as a result.
</p>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_7572B">--</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7572B = class {
  #key = "el_signal_7572B";

  async test_signal_7572B(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    const options = {
      headers: {
        "x-bitty-test": "data_signal_7572B",
      },
    };
    // await this.fetchElement(key, url, fallback, options);
    //
    // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
    // This test is set to always pass as a result.
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_7572B");
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

--
Use a POST call to fetch an Element. [0090-with-post]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class6B398"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have
  the ability to modify responses based on 
  request options. Verification is done manually. 
  This automated test is set to always pass
  as a result.
</p>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_6B398">--</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class6B398 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_6B398";

  async test_signal_6B398(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    const options = {
      method: "POST",
    };
    // await this.fetchElement(key, url, fallback, options);
    //
    // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
    // This test is set to always pass as a result.
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_6B398");
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

--
Notes
  • TODO: Display parsing HTML Footnote
fetchFragment(key, url, fallback = null, options = {}) [Done]
Description
  • Fetch a document fragment from a URL with a given `key`.
  • The fragment can be used with the related methods: `updateFragment()`, `renderFragment()`, `deleteFragment()`.
Examples
Fetch a fragment from a URL [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE47B8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E47B8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE47B8 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_E47B8";

  async test_signal_E47B8(url, el) {
    await this.fetchFragment(this.#key, url);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E47B8");
  }

  given_signal_E47B8(_, __) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
    this.send(url, "test_signal_E47B8");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
The fragment is saved to storage if the fetch succeeds. [0040-saved-to-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class0CCFB"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_0CCFB">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class0CCFB = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_0CCFB";

  test_signal_0CCFB(_, el) {
    this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_0CCFB");
  }

  async given_signal_0CCFB(_, __) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
    await this.fetchFragment(this.#key, url);
    delete this._fragment[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_0CCFB");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Return ok as true if the fetch succeeded. [0050-ok-if-success]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBE8EB"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_BE8EB">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBE8EB = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_BE8EB";

  async test_signal_BE8EB(url, el) {
    const result = await this.fetchFragment(this.#key, url);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_BE8EB");
  }

  given_signal_BE8EB(_, __) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
    this.send(url, "test_signal_BE8EB");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return error if there's a problem with the fetch. [0055-error-if-problem]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC7878"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: This test causes expected error messages in the
  console. To avoid noise during the initial load, this
  test is run manually.
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_C7878">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_C7878">waiting</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC7878 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_C7878";

  async test_signal_C7878(url, el) {
    const result = await this.fetchFragment(this.#key, url);
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  run_signal_C7878(_, __) {
    const url = "/non-exising-page.html";
    this.send(url, "test_signal_C7878");
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: This test causes expected error messages in the console. To avoid noise during the initial load, this test is run manually.

waiting
Log and return a warning (but still ok === true) if fetch overwrites an existing fragment. [0060-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF9E7D"></bitty-8-0>

JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF9E7D = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_F9E7D";

  async test_signal_F9E7D(url, el) {
    const result = await this.fetchFragment(this.#key, url);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_F9E7D");

  given_signal_F9E7D(_, __) {

bug
Fallback to a string if fetch fails. [0070-string-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF3B99"></bitty-8-0>
<p>NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. 
  It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_F3B99">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_F3B99">waiting</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF3B99 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_F3B99";

  async test_signal_F3B99(payload, el) {
    await this.fetchFragment(this.#key, payload.url, payload.fallback);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  run_signal_F3B99(_, __) {
    const fallback = "<div></div><div>ok</div>";
    const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
    this.send(
      { url: url, fallback: fallback },
      "test_signal_F3B99",
    );
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load

waiting
Use a fallback element if fetch fails. [0072-element-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class11320"></bitty-8-0>
<p>NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. 
  It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_11320">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_11320">waiting</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class11320 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_11320";

  async test_signal_11320(payload, el) {
    await this.fetchFragment(this.#key, payload.url, payload.fallback);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[0].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  run_signal_11320(_, __) {
    const fallbackElement = document.createElement("div");
    fallbackElement.innerHTML = "ok";
    const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
    this.send(
      { url: url, fallback: fallbackElement },
      "test_signal_11320",
    );
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load

waiting
Use a document fragment fallback if fetch fails. [0075-fragment-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class80552"></bitty-8-0>
<p>NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. 
  It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_80552">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_80552">waiting</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class80552 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_80552";

  async test_signal_80552(payload, el) {
    await this.fetchFragment(this.#key, payload.url, payload.fallback);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[0].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  run_signal_80552(_, __) {
    const fallbackTemplate = document.createElement("template");
    fallbackTemplate.innerHTML = "<div>ok</div>";
    const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
    this.send(
      { url: url, fallback: fallbackTemplate.content },
      "test_signal_80552",
    );
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load

waiting
Warn if a fallback was used. [0078-warn-if-fallback-used]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassEEB5B"></bitty-8-0>
<p>NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. 
  It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_EEB5B">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_EEB5B">waiting</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassEEB5B = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_EEB5B";

  async test_signal_EEB5B(payload, el) {
    const result = await this.fetchFragment(
      this.#key,
      payload.url,
      payload.fallback,
    );
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    } else {
      el.innerHTML = "bug";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  run_signal_EEB5B(_, __) {
    const fallbackTemplate = document.createElement("template");
    fallbackTemplate.innerHTML = "<div>ok</div>";
    const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.html";
    this.send(
      { url: url, fallback: fallbackTemplate.content },
      "test_signal_EEB5B",
    );
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: This tests causes expected errors in the console. It's run manually to avoid noise on the initial page load

waiting
Pass fetch options. [0080-use-options]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class6F792"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: The serve this site uses ignores extra fetch options. 
  This test shows how to add fetch options 
  but it's not tested here automatically. 
</p>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_6F792"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class6F792 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_6F792";

  async test_signal_6F792(url, el) {
    const options = {
      fetchOptions: {
        headers: {
          "x-bitty-test": "key_signal_6F792",
        },
      },
    };
    await this.fetchFragment(this.#key, url, null, options);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
    this.send(url, "test_signal_6F792");
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: The serve this site uses ignores extra fetch options. This test shows how to add fetch options but it's not tested here automatically.

Make a POST request. [0090-post-call]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBC346"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: 
  The serve this site uses does not accept POST
  requests. This test shows how POST requests can be made
  but it's not tested here automatically. 
</p>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_BC346"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBC346 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_BC346";

  async test_signal_BC346(_, el) {
    const url = "https://www.example.com";
    const options = {
      fetchOptions: {
        method: "POST",
      },
    };
    // The server this site uses doesn't accept
    // POST Requests. This is the line that
    // would make one:
    //
    // await this.fetchFragment(key, url, null, options);
    //
    // Since the server would reject it, it's
    // not tested here.
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_BC346");
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: The serve this site uses does not accept POST requests. This test shows how POST requests can be made but it's not tested here automatically.

fetchJSON(key, url [, options]) [TODO - Add fallback]
Description
  • Fetch a JSON from a URL, add it to `this.json[key]`, and store a copy in localStorage
Examples
Fetching a JSON with `this.fetchJSON(key, url)` stores it in `this.json[key]`. [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class125F4"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_125F4">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class125F4 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_125F4");
  }

  given_signal_125F4(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_125F4");
  }

  async test_signal_125F4(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
    await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_125F4", url);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_125F4"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Pass fetch options with `this.fetchJSON(key, url, options)`. [0020-pass-options]

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7878C"></bitty-8-0>

  as a result.

    this.trigger("given_signal_7878C");
  }

  given_signal_7878C(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_7878C");
  }

  async test_signal_7878C(_, el) {

      },
    };

    //
    // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
    // This test is set to always pass as a result.
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>

JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF8955 = class {

    this.trigger("given_signal_F8955");
  }

  given_signal_F8955(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_F8955");
  }

  async test_signal_F8955(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";

      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }

bug
Fetched items are added to localStorage. [0035-add-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCEAF0"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_CEAF0">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCEAF0 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_CEAF0");
  }

  async given_signal_CEAF0(_, __) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
    await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_CEAF0", url);
    this.trigger("test_signal_CEAF0");
  }

  test_signal_CEAF0(_, el) {
    const verify = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("data_signal_CEAF0")).data;
    el.innerHTML = verify.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return error with `ok` is `false` when there's a problem with the fetch call. The full response object is dumped to `console.error()`. The primary strings from the response object are added to the `extraInfo` key in value returned from `fetchJSON()`. [0040-fetch-issue]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5862A"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: Fetch errors show up in the console. To avoid
  adding noise to the documentation page this test
  is therefor run manually. 
</p>
<button data-send="test_signal_5862A">Test Manually</button>
<pre class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_5862A">waiting</pre>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5862A = class {
  async test_signal_5862A(_, el) {
    const url = "/file-that-does-not-exist.json";
    const result = await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_5862A", url);
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = JSON.stringify(result, null, 2);
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: Fetch errors show up in the console. To avoid adding noise to the documentation page this test is therefor run manually. 

waiting
Log and return error with `ok` is `false` when the JSON can't be parsed. [0050-parse-issue]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class4734F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_4734F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class4734F = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_4734F");
  }

  given_signal_4734F(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_4734F");
  }

  async test_signal_4734F(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/invalid-json.xjson";
    const result = await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_4734F", url);
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return warning with `ok` is `true` when an existing `this.json[key]` is overwritten. [0060-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class78263"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_78263">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class78263 = class {

    this.trigger("given_signal_78263");
  }

  given_signal_78263(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.addJSON("data_signal_78263", {});
    this.trigger("test_signal_78263");
  }

  async test_signal_78263(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
    const result = await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_78263", url);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
fetchSVG(key, url, fallback = null, options = {})
Description
Examples
Fetch an SVG from a URL. [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class74651"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_74651">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_74651"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class74651 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_74651";

  async test_signal_74651(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-svg.svg";
    await this.fetchSVG(this.#key, url);
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_74651");
  }

  view_signal_74651(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_74651");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
`fetchSVG()` has a return value object with `.ok === true` when the fetch succeeds. [0030-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA9C93"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A9C93">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_A9C93"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA9C93 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_A9C93";

  async test_signal_A9C93(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-svg.svg";
    const result = await this.fetchSVG(this.#key, url);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
    this.trigger("view_signal_A9C93");
  }

  view_signal_A9C93(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(this.renderSVG(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_A9C93");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Fetched SVGs are added to storage. [0035-add-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB6D8D"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_B6D8D">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_B6D8D"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB6D8D = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_B6D8D";

  async test_signal_B6D8D(_, el) {
    this.loadSVG(this.#key);
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_B6D8D");
  }

  view_signal_B6D8D(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  async bittyReady() {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-svg.svg";
    await this.fetchSVG(this.#key, url);
    delete this._svg[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_B6D8D");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Return a result with `level === "error"` and `ok === false` if there's an issue with the fetch. [0040-fetch-issue]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9F02E"></bitty-8-0>
<p>NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in 
  the console. The test is run manually to prevent
  the noise from showing up on initial page load.</p>
<button data-send="signal_9F02E">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_9F02E">waiting</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_9F02E"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9F02E = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_9F02E";

  async signal_9F02E(_, el) {
    const url = "/intentionally-missing-file.svg";
    const result = await this.fetchSVG(this.#key, url);
    if (result.level === "error" && result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in the console. The test is run manually to prevent the noise from showing up on initial page load.

waiting
If a fetch overwrites an existing SVG a warning is logged and returned with `level === "warn"` and `ok === true`. [0060-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB1D68"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_B1D68">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_B1D68"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB1D68 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_B1D68";

  async test_signal_B1D68(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-svg.svg";
    const result = await this.fetchSVG(this.#key, url);
    if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
    this.trigger("view_signal_B1D68");
  }

  view_signal_B1D68(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(this.renderSVG(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    this.trigger("test_signal_B1D68");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
