getLogLevel() [Done]
Description
Examples
Return the log level string. [0010-return-log-level-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE1B2F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E1B2F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE1B2F = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E1B2F");
  }

  given_signal_E1B2F(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_E1B2F");
  }

  async test_signal_E1B2F(_, el) {
    const logLevel = this.getLogLevel();
    if (logLevel === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
json[key] [Done]
Description
  • A collection of JSON objects stored as values for a given `key`.
  • JSONs can be loaded via `addJSON()`, `fetchJSON()`, `loadJSON()` or from script tags with `type="application/json"` and a `data-key` (see TKTKTK section below for more details).
Examples
A basic `addJSON(key, data)` example. See the individual documentation for more details on all the methods. [0010-add-json-example]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE70E0"></bitty-8-0>

<script type="application/json" data-key="data_signal_E70E0">
  { "status": "ok" }
</script>

<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E70E0">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE70E0 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E70E0");
  }

  given_signal_E70E0(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_E70E0");
  }

  test_signal_E70E0(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_E70E0"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
loadElement(key, fallback = null) [Done]
Description
  • Load an element from storage into `this.element[key]`.
  • Use the optional `fallback` if there's no element with the given `key` in storage
Examples
Use `this.loadElement(key)` to load an element from storage into `this.element[key]`. [0010-basic-load]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class23191"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_23191">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class23191 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_23191";

  test_signal_23191(_, el) {
    this.loadElement(this.#key);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
    delete this.element[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_23191");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an `ok` result with `true` if the load succeeds. [0012-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBB0D2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_BB0D2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBB0D2 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_BB0D2";

  test_signal_BB0D2(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadElement(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    delete this._element[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_BB0D2");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Overwrites an existing `this.element[key]` if one exists. Logs and returns a warning of it does so (the `ok` value is still `true`). [0015-overwirtes-existing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassFFDD5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_FFDD5">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassFFDD5 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_FFDD5";

  test_signal_FFDD5(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadElement(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_FFDD5");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Strings can be used as fallbacks. [0020-fallback-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB86EE"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_B86EE">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB86EE = class {
  #key = "el_signal_B86EE";

  test_signal_B86EE(_, el) {
    this.loadElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_B86EE");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Element fallbacks are used. [0030-fallback-element]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class31037"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_31037">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class31037 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_31037";

  test_signal_31037(_, el) {
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.classList.add("test");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.loadElement(this.#key, newEl);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_31037");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Document Fragment fallbacks can be used for fallbacks. [0031-fallback-fragment]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBC589"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_BC589">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBC589 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_BC589";

  test_signal_BC589(_, el) {
    const template = document.createElement("template");
    template.innerHTML = `<div class="test">ok</div>`;
    this.loadElement(this.#key, template.content);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_BC589");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Only the first element from the root of a fallback document fragment is used. Any siblings are dropped. [0032-fragmnets-truncate]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class77D07"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_77D07">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class77D07 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_77D07";

  test_signal_77D07(_, el) {
    const template = document.createElement("template");
    template.innerHTML = `<div></div><div></div>`;
    const result = this.loadElement(this.#key, template.content);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_77D07");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Fallbacks are added to storage when they are used. [0033-string-stored-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9D350"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_9D350">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9D350 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_9D350";

  test_signal_9D350(_, el) {
    this.loadElement(this.#key);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.deleteElement(this.#key);
    this.loadElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
    delete this._element[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_9D350");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an error if there if the `key` doesn't exist in storage and there's no `fallback` [0040-fail-missing-key]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class94D78"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_94D78">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class94D78 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_94D78";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_94D78");
  }

  given_signal_94D78(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.deleteElement("el_signal_94D78");
    this.trigger("test_signal_94D78");
  }

  test_signal_94D78(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadElement("el_signal_94D78");
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
loadFragment(key, fallback = null) [Done]
Description
Examples
Load from storage. [0010-basic-load]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5D60F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_5D60F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5D60F = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_5D60F";

  test_signal_5D60F(_, el) {
    this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_5D60F");
  }

  given_signal_5D60F(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, "<div>ok</div>");
    delete this._fragment[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_5D60F");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Report ok on success. [0015-ok-on-success]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class697D1"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_697D1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class697D1 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_697D1";

  test_signal_697D1(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_697D1");
  }

  given_signal_697D1(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    delete this._fragment[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_697D1");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Error if key doesn't exist and there's no fallback. [0017-error-if-no-key-or-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7EF03"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_7EF03">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7EF03 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_7EF03";

  test_signal_7EF03(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_7EF03");
  }

  given_signal_7EF03(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_7EF03");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Warn if overwriting an existing fragment. [0020-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF8382"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_F8382">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF8382 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_F8382";

  test_signal_F8382(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_F8382");
  }

  given_signal_F8382(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_F8382");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use string for a fallback. [0030-string-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class38302"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_38302">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class38302 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_38302";

  test_signal_38302(_, el) {
    this.loadFragment(this.#key, `<div></div><div>ok</div>`);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_38302");
  }

  given_signal_38302(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_38302");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use element for a fallback. [0040-element-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5A3FB"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_5A3FB">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5A3FB = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_5A3FB";

  test_signal_5A3FB(newElement, el) {
    this.loadFragment(this.#key, newElement);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[0].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_5A3FB");
  }

  given_signal_5A3FB(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    const newElement = document.createElement("div");
    newElement.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.send(newElement, "test_signal_5A3FB");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use fragment for a fallback. [0050-fragment-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class119D0"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_119D0">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class119D0 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_119D0";

  test_signal_119D0(fallbackFragment, el) {
    this.loadFragment(this.#key, fallbackFragment);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_119D0");
  }

  given_signal_119D0(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    const fallbackFragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const fallbackElement1 = document.createElement("div");
    const fallbackElement2 = document.createElement("div");
    fallbackElement2.innerHTML = "ok";
    fallbackFragment.appendChild(fallbackElement1);
    fallbackFragment.appendChild(fallbackElement2);
    this.send(fallbackFragment, "test_signal_119D0");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Error if a fallback is used but it's invalid. [0055-error-invalid-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class99F17"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_99F17">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class99F17 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_99F17";

  test_signal_99F17(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadFragment(this.#key, {
      misc: "objects are not valid fallbacks",
    });
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_99F17");
  }

  given_signal_99F17(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_99F17");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Fallbacks are stored automatically. [0060-fallbacks-stored]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassD027C"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_D027C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassD027C = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_D027C";

  test_signal_D027C(fallbackFragment, el) {
    this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.renderFragment(this.#key).firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_D027C");
  }

  given_signal_D027C(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.loadFragment(this.#key, "<div>ok</div>");
    delete this._fragment[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_D027C");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
loadJSON(key [, fallback]) [Done]
Description

Load a JSON object into the `this.json[key]` collection.

Examples
Use `this.loadJSON(key)` to load a JSON into `this.json[key]`. [0010-json-exists-in-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC89F4"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_C89F4">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC89F4 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_C89F4");
  }

  given_signal_C89F4(_, __) {
    this.addJSON("json_signal_C89F4", `{ "status": "ok" }`);
    delete this.json["json_signal_C89F4"];
    this.trigger("test_signal_C89F4");
  }

  test_signal_C89F4(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadJSON("json_signal_C89F4");
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = this.json["json_signal_C89F4"].status;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
`this.loadJSON(key, fallback)` overwrites an existing `this.json[key]` value if it exists. [0015-overwirte-existing-value]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class92D8A"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_92D8A">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class92D8A = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_92D8A");
  }

  given_signal_92D8A(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.addJSON(
      "json_signal_92D8A",
      { status: "ok" },
    );
    this.trigger("test_signal_92D8A");
  }

  test_signal_92D8A(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadJSON("json_signal_92D8A");
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = this.json["json_signal_92D8A"].status;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use `this.loadJSON(key, fallback)` to load a fallback from a string if the key doesn't exist. [0020-fallback-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCEE9A"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_CEE9A">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCEE9A = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_CEE9A");
  }

  given_signal_CEE9A(_, __) {
    localStorage.removeItem("data_signal_CEE9A");
    this.trigger("test_signal_CEE9A");
  }

  test_signal_CEE9A(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadJSON("data_signal_CEE9A", `{ "status": "ok" }`);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_CEE9A"].status;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use `this.loadJSON(key, fallback)` to load a fallback from an object if the key doesn't exist. [0030-fallback-object]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class230A6"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_230A6">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class230A6 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_230A6");
  }

  given_signal_230A6(_, __) {
    localStorage.removeItem("data_signal_230A6");
    this.trigger("test_signal_230A6");
  }

  test_signal_230A6(_, el) {
    const fallback = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
    const result = this.loadJSON("data_signal_230A6", fallback);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_230A6"].status;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
If a `fallback` string is used it's automatically saved back into localStorage. [0033-string-save-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE6EB8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E6EB8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE6EB8 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E6EB8");
  }

  given_signal_E6EB8(_, __) {
    const fallback = `{ "status": "ok" }`;
    const result = this.loadJSON("data_signal_E6EB8", fallback);
    this.trigger("test_signal_E6EB8");
  }

  test_signal_E6EB8(_, el) {
    const storage = localStorage.getItem("data_signal_E6EB8");
    el.innerHTML = JSON.parse(storage).data.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
If a `fallback` object is used it's automatically saved back into localStorage. [0035-object-save-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA3FE1"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A3FE1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA3FE1 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A3FE1");
  }

  given_signal_A3FE1(_, __) {
    const fallback = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
    const result = this.loadJSON("data_signal_A3FE1", fallback);
    this.trigger("test_signal_A3FE1");
  }

  test_signal_A3FE1(_, el) {
    const storage = localStorage.getItem("data_signal_A3FE1");
    el.innerHTML = JSON.parse(storage).data.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Return an error if the `key` doesn't exist and there's no `fallback`. [0040-missing-key-fails]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class194AD"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_194AD">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class194AD = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_194AD");
  }

  given_signal_194AD(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    localStorage.removeItem("missing_key_signal_194AD");
    this.trigger("test_signal_194AD");
  }

  test_signal_194AD(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadJSON("missing_key_signal_194AD");
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Return an error if the `key` doesn't exist and the `fallback` JSON is a string that fails to parse. [0050-error-if-parse-fails]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF4560"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_F4560">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF4560 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_F4560");
  }

  given_signal_F4560(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_F4560");
  }

  test_signal_F4560(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadJSON("missing_key_signal_F4560", "invalid json");
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Notes
  • The JSON object is pulled from a `data` key in the localStorage object (i.e. `{ "data": JSON_CONTENT }`.
  • The top level `data` key is what's produces from `this.saveJSON(key)`.
  • The `fallback` value is used if the `key` doesn't exist.
  • Note that `fallback` JSON should not add the top level `data` key. It's added and removed automatically by the `loadJSON` and `saveJSON` functions.
loadSVG(key, fallback = null)
Description
Examples
Load from storage. [0010-basic-load]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class2D8D5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_2D8D5">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_2D8D5"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class2D8D5 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_2D8D5";

  test_signal_2D8D5(_, el) {
    this.loadSVG(this.#key);
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_2D8D5");
  }

  view_signal_2D8D5(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_2D8D5");
  }

  given_signal_2D8D5(_, __) {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    delete this._svg[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_2D8D5");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Report ok on success. [0015-ok-on-success]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class8F8DD"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_8F8DD">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_8F8DD"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class8F8DD = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_8F8DD";

  test_signal_8F8DD(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadSVG(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
    this.trigger("view_signal_8F8DD");
  }

  view_signal_8F8DD(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(this.renderSVG(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_8F8DD");
  }

  given_signal_8F8DD(_, __) {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeSVG(this.#key);
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    delete this._svg[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_8F8DD");
  }
};
</script>
Results

      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

    this.trigger("given_signal_DB540");
  }

  given_signal_DB540(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeSVG(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_DB540");
  }
};

      el.innerHTML = "ok";

    this.trigger("given_signal_2AB87");

<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    this.trigger("test_signal_2AB87");

};

Use string for a fallback. [0030-string-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class655D9"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_655D9">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_655D9"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class655D9 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_655D9";

  test_signal_655D9(fallback, el) {
    this.loadSVG(this.#key, fallback);
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_655D9");
  }

  view_signal_655D9(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_655D9");
  }

  given_signal_655D9(_, __) {
    const fallback = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeSVG(this.#key);
    this.send(fallback, "test_signal_655D9");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use SVG for a fallback. [0050-svg-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCEDB1"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_CEDB1">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_CEDB1"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCEDB1 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_CEDB1";

  test_signal_CEDB1(fallback, el) {
    this.loadSVG(this.#key, fallback);
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_CEDB1");
  }

  view_signal_CEDB1(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_CEDB1");
  }

  given_signal_CEDB1(_, __) {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    const template = document.createElement("template");
    template.innerHTML = input;
    const fallback = template.content.querySelector("svg");
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeSVG(this.#key);
    this.send(fallback, "test_signal_CEDB1");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Error if a fallback is used but it's invalid. [0055-error-invalid-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class609BD"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_609BD">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class609BD = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_609BD";

  test_signal_609BD(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadSVG(this.#key, {
      misc: "objects are not valid fallbacks",
    });
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_609BD");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Fallbacks are stored automatically. [0060-fallbacks-stored]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB2E8A"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_B2E8A">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_B2E8A"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB2E8A = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_B2E8A";

  test_signal_B2E8A(_, el) {
    this.loadSVG(this.#key);
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_B2E8A");
  }

  view_signal_B2E8A(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeSVG(this.#key);
    this.loadSVG(this.#key, input);
    delete this._svg[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_B2E8A");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
removeJSON(key) [Done]
Description
  • Removes `this.json[key]`.
Examples
Use `this.removeJSON(key)` to remove an existing `this.json[key]` item. [0010-removing-existing-json]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class926B8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_926B8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class926B8 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_926B8");
  }

  given_signal_926B8(_, __) {
    this.addJSON("data_signal_926B8", `{}`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_926B8");
  }

  test_signal_926B8(_, el) {
    this.removeJSON("data_signal_926B8");
    if (this.json["data_signal_926B8"] === undefined) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
JSON is removed from localStorage as well. [0015-remove-from-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class57CC1"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_57CC1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class57CC1 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_57CC1");
  }

  given_signal_57CC1(_, __) {
    this.addJSON("data_signal_57CC1", `{}`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_57CC1");
  }

  test_signal_57CC1(_, el) {
    this.removeJSON("data_signal_57CC1");
    const checkStorage = localStorage.getItem("data_signal_57CC1");
    if (checkStorage === null) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return `ok` is `true` if item removed. [0020-return-ok-if-removed]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5E09F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_5E09F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5E09F = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_5E09F");
  }

  given_signal_5E09F(_, __) {
    this.addJSON("data_signal_5E09F", `{}`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_5E09F");
  }

  test_signal_5E09F(_, el) {
    const result = this.removeJSON("data_signal_5E09F");
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return warning with `ok` is `true` if the key did not exist. [0030-warn-if-key-did-not-exist]

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1C2EF"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_1C2EF">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class1C2EF = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_1C2EF");
  }

  given_signal_1C2EF(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_1C2EF");
  }

  test_signal_1C2EF(_, el) {
    const result = this.removeJSON("data_signal_1C2EF");
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>

bug
renderElement(key, substitutions = {}) [Done]
Description
Examples
basic render with no subs [0010-basic-render]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class3EB4D"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_3EB4D">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class3EB4D = class {
  #key = "el_signal_3EB4D";

  test_signal_3EB4D(_, el) {
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_3EB4D");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Undefined if the key doesn't exist. (An error is added to the logs) [0015-undefined-if-no-key]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class30B5D"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_30B5D">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class30B5D = class {
  #key = "el_signal_30B5D";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_30B5D");
  }

  given_signal_30B5D(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_30B5D");
  }

  test_signal_30B5D(_, el) {
    if (
      this.renderElement(this.#key) === undefined &&
      this.logs[0].level === "error"
    ) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
String text replacements. [0020-text-single-subs]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class3ECFF"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_3ECFF">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class3ECFF = class {
  #key = "el_signal_3ECFF";

  test_signal_3ECFF(_, el) {
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_3ECFF": "ok",
    };
    el.replaceWith(
      this.renderElement(this.#key, subs),
    );
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">TARGET_3ECFF</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_3ECFF");
  }
};
</script>

HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class317AA"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_317AA">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class317AA = class {
  #key = "el_signal_317AA";

  test_signal_317AA(_, el) {
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_317AA": ["o", "k"],
    };
    el.replaceWith(
      this.renderElement(this.#key, subs),
    );
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">TARGET_317AA</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_317AA");
  }
};

Results
bug
Substitute elements. [0030-element-sub]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassAB8CB"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_AB8CB">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassAB8CB = class {
  #key = "el_signal_AB8CB";

  test_signal_AB8CB(_, el) {
    const replacementEl = document.createElement("div");
    replacementEl.classList.add("test");
    replacementEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_AB8CB": replacementEl,
    };
    el.replaceWith(
      this.renderElement(this.#key, subs),
    );
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div>TARGET_AB8CB</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_AB8CB");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use an array of element for substitution. [0035-array-elements]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF7F0A"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_F7F0A">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF7F0A = class {
  #key = "el_signal_F7F0A";

  test_signal_F7F0A(_, el) {
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_F7F0A": [
        this.renderElement(`replace1_F7F0A`),
        this.renderElement(`replace2_F7F0A`),
      ],
    };
    el.replaceWith(
      this.renderElement(this.#key, subs),
    );
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div>TARGET_F7F0A</div>`);
    this.createElement(`replace1_F7F0A`, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
    this.createElement(`replace2_F7F0A`, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_F7F0A");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use a document fragment for a substitution. [0040-fragment-single]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class00B6E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_00B6E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class00B6E = class {
  #key = "el_signal_00B6E";

  test_signal_00B6E(_, el) {
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_00B6E": this.renderFragment(`replacement_00B6E`),
    };
    el.replaceWith(
      this.renderElement(this.#key, subs),
    );
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div>TARGET_00B6E</div>`);
    this.createFragment(
      `replacement_00B6E`,
      `<div class="test">ok</div><div class="test">ok</div>`,
    );
    this.trigger("test_signal_00B6E");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use an array of document fragments for a replacement. [0045-fragment-array]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class81AA3"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_81AA3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class81AA3 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_81AA3";

  test_signal_81AA3(_, el) {
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_81AA3": [
        this.renderFragment(`replacement1_81AA3`),
        this.renderFragment(`replacement2_81AA3`),
      ],
    };
    el.replaceWith(
      this.renderElement(this.#key, subs),
    );
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div>TARGET_81AA3</div>`);
    this.createFragment(
      `replacement1_81AA3`,
      `<div class="test">ok</div><div class="test">ok</div>`,
    );
    this.createFragment(
      `replacement2_81AA3`,
      `<div class="test">ok</div><div class="test">ok</div>`,
    );
    this.trigger("test_signal_81AA3");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
renderFragment(key, substitutions = {}) [Done]
Description
  • Returns a new instance of the requested document fragment for the given `key`.
  • Find and replacement is done with the optional substitutions if they are supplied.
  • The values of substitutions can be: a string, an array of strings, an element, an array of elements, a document fragment, or an array of document fragments.
Examples
Render with no substitutions. [0010-no-subs]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassFC310"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_FC310">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassFC310 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_FC310";

  test_signal_FC310(_, el) {
    const fragment = this.renderFragment(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = fragment.children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_FC310");
  }

  given_signal_FC310(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div></div><div>ok</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_FC310");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use single strings for substitutions. [0020-string-sub]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1BEA7"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_1BEA7">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>

  #key = "fragment_signal_1BEA7";

    const subs = {
      "TARGET_1BEA7": "ok",
    };
    const fragment = this.renderFragment(this.#key, subs);
    el.innerHTML = fragment.children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

    this.trigger("given_signal_1BEA7");
  }

  given_signal_1BEA7(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div></div><div>TARGET_1BEA7</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_1BEA7");
  }
};
</script>

bug
Use an element for substitutions. [0030-element-sub]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class31065"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_31065">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class31065 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_31065";

  test_signal_31065(_, el) {
    const replacementEl = document.createElement("div");
    replacementEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_31065": replacementEl,
    };
    const fragment = this.renderFragment(this.#key, subs);
    el.innerHTML = fragment.firstChild.firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_31065");
  }

  given_signal_31065(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div>TARGET_31065</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_31065");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use an array of elements for substitutions. [0035-array-elements]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA86C1"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A86C1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA86C1 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_A86C1";

  test_signal_A86C1(_, el) {
    const replacementEl = document.createElement("div");
    replacementEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    const replacementEl2 = document.createElement("div");
    replacementEl2.innerHTML = "ok";
    const replacementArray = [
      replacementEl,
      replacementEl2,
    ];
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_A86C1": replacementArray,
    };
    const fragment = this.renderFragment(this.#key, subs);
    el.innerHTML = fragment.firstChild.children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A86C1");
  }

  given_signal_A86C1(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div>TARGET_A86C1</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_A86C1");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use a document fragment for substitutions. [0040-fragment-sub]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF7489"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_F7489">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF7489 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_F7489";

  test_signal_F7489(_, el) {
    const template = document.createElement("template");
    template.innerHTML = "<div></div><div>ok</div>";
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_F7489": template.content,
    };
    const fragment = this.renderFragment(this.#key, subs);
    el.innerHTML = fragment.firstChild.children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_F7489");
  }

  given_signal_F7489(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div>TARGET_F7489</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_F7489");
  }
};
</script>

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC24F9"></bitty-8-0>

JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC24F9 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_C24F9";

  test_signal_C24F9(_, el) {
    const template = document.createElement("template");
    template.innerHTML = "<div></div><div></div>";
    const template2 = document.createElement("template");
    template2.innerHTML = "<div></div><div>ok</div>";
    const replacementArray = [
      template.content,
      template2.content,
    ];
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_C24F9": replacementArray,
    };
    const fragment = this.renderFragment(this.#key, subs);
    el.innerHTML = fragment.firstChild.children[3].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_C24F9");
  }

  given_signal_C24F9(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createFragment(this.#key, `<div>TARGET_C24F9</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_C24F9");
  }
};
</script>

bug
Return undefined if the key doesn't exist. [0090-no-key-returns-undefined]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassAD946"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_AD946">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassAD946 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_AD946";

  test_signal_AD946(_, el) {
    const fragment = this.renderFragment(this.#key);
    if (fragment === undefined) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_AD946");
  }

  given_signal_AD946(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_AD946");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log an error if trying to render a fragment that doesn't exist. [0095-log-no-key]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class88717"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_88717">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class88717 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_88717";

  test_signal_88717(_, el) {
    this.logs = [];
    this.renderFragment(this.#key);
    if (this.logs[0].ok === false && this.logs[0].level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_88717");
  }

  given_signal_88717(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_88717");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
renderSVG(key, substitutions = {})
Description
Render an SVG with or without substitutions.
Examples
Render with no substitutions. [0010-no-subs]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class57C32"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_57C32">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_57C32"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class57C32 = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_57C32";

  test_signal_57C32(_, el) {
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_57C32");
  }

  view_signal_57C32(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    this.trigger("test_signal_57C32");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use single strings for substitutions. [0020-string-sub]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class4375F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_4375F">bug</div>
<div data-receive="view_signal_4375F"></div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class4375F = class {
  #key = "svg_signal_4375F";

  test_signal_4375F(_, el) {
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_4375F": "ok",
    };
    const svg = this.renderSVG(this.#key, subs);
    el.innerHTML = svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.send(svg, "view_signal_4375F");
  }

  view_signal_4375F(svg, el) {
    el.replaceWith(svg);
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    const input = `
<svg version="1.1" width="60" height="40" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="30" y="24" font-size="20" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">TARGET_4375F</text>
</svg>`;
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createSVG(this.#key, input);
    this.trigger("test_signal_4375F");
  }
};
</script>

bug
saveJSON(key) [Done]
Description
  • Saves the JSON with the given `key` into localStorage
Examples
Save the requested key. [0010-save-valid-key]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE097F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E097F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE097F = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E097F");
  }

  given_signal_E097F(_, __) {
    this.json["data_signal_E097F"] = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_E097F");
  }

  test_signal_E097F(_, el) {
    this.saveJSON("data_signal_E097F");
    const verify = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("data_signal_E097F"));
    el.innerHTML = verify.data.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
`saveJSON(key)` logs and returns ok if it's successful [0015-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassAF434"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_AF434">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassAF434 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_AF434");
  }

  given_signal_AF434(_, __) {
    this.json["data_signal_AF434"] = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_AF434");
  }

  test_signal_AF434(_, el) {
    const result = this.saveJSON("data_signal_AF434");
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Error if the requested key does not exist. [0020-no-key-error]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class0E048"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_0E048">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class0E048 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_0E048");
  }

  given_signal_0E048(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_0E048");
  }

  test_signal_0E048(_, el) {
    const result = this.saveJSON("no-key-with-this-name");
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Invalid JSONs that don't parse return an error and do not save [0030-parse-errors-do-not-save]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class47EC2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_47EC2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class47EC2 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_47EC2");
  }

  given_signal_47EC2(_, __) {
    this.json["invalid_json"] = "not json";
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_47EC2");
  }

  test_signal_47EC2(_, el) {
    const result = this.saveJSON("invalid_json");
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
send(payload, signals) [Done]
Description
  • Used to send payloads to trigger signals.
Examples
Send a single signal with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` to a single receiver. [0010-send-a-basic-signal]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassDFC26"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_DFC26">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassDFC26 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_DFC26");
  }

  signal_DFC26(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Send a single signal with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` to multiple receivers. [0015-send-signal-with-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class138D4"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_138D4">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_138D4">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_138D4">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class138D4 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_138D4");
  }

  signal_138D4(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Send multiple signals to a single receiver with `this.api.set(payload, signals)` [0020-send-multiple-signals-to-single-receiver]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class20C90"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_20C90 signal_20C90_2 signal_20C90_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class20C90 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.send(
      { status: "ok" },
      `signal_20C90 signal_20C90_2 signal_20C90_3`,
    );
  }

  signal_20C90(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = `alfa-${payload.status}`;
  }

  signal_20C90_2(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = `${el.innerHTML}-bravo-${payload.status}`;
  }

  signal_20C90_3(payload, el) {
    if (payload.status === "ok" && el.innerHTML === "alfa-ok-bravo-ok") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Send multiple signals to multiple receivers with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` [0025-multiple-signals-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCE3AE"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CE3AE">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CE3AE_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CE3AE_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCE3AE = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.send(
      { status: "ok" },
      `signal_CE3AE signal_CE3AE_2 signal_CE3AE_3`,
    );
  }

  signal_CE3AE(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }

  signal_CE3AE_2(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }

  signal_CE3AE_3(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Send a string. [0027-send-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class31AF8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_31AF8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class31AF8 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.send("ok", "signal_31AF8");
  }

  signal_31AF8(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Send a signal to a method that doesn't have a corresponding receiver with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` [0030-send-signals-to-method-without-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1EF08"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_1EF08">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class1EF08 = class {
  #_elIsNull = false;
  #_status;

  bittyReady() {
    this.send({ status: "ok" }, "test_signal_1EF08");
  }

  test_signal_1EF08(payload, x) {
    this.#_status = payload.status;
    if (x === null) {
      this.#_elIsNull = true;
    }
    this.trigger("verify_signal_1EF08");
  }

  verify_signal_1EF08(_, el) {
    if (this.#_elIsNull === true) {
      el.innerHTML = this.#_status;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Signals sent with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` in one bit are received in others. [0040-signals-sent-from-one-bit-are-received-in-others]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF8EF8"></bitty-8-0>
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF8EF8_2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F8EF8_2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF8EF8 = class {
  async bittyReady() {
    // delay to let other bit initialize
    await this.sleep(100);
    this.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_F8EF8_2");
  }
};

window.ClassF8EF8_2 = class {
  signal_F8EF8_2(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Await signals inside a string of `this.api.send(payload, signals)` (e.g. `this.api.send({status: "ok", "}, "await:SIG1 SIG2")`) [0070-await-individual-signals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class75189"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_75189">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class75189 = class {
  #t1;

  bittyReady() {
    this.#t1 = performance.now();
    this.send(
      { status: "ok" },
      "await:test_signal_75189 verify_signal_75189",
    );
  }

  async test_signal_75189(payload, el) {
    await this.sleep(50);
  }

  verify_signal_75189(payload, el) {
    const t2 = performance.now();
    if ((t2 - this.#t1) > 40) {
      el.innerHTML = payload.status;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Adding an `await` to `this.api.send()` has no practical effect. The api method fires an event that can be picked up by any number of signal methods inside any number of instances from any number of bits. No attempt is made to try to coordinate async/await across all the potential variations. [0071-awaiting-send-does-nothing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class94BDF"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_94BDF">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class94BDF = class {
  #_updated = false;
  #_t1;

  async bittyReady() {
    this.#_t1 = performance.now();
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  THIS DOES NOT WORK AS YOU MIGHT EXPECT  //
    //  Read the description above for why.     //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    await this.send({ updated: true }, "await:test_signal_94BDF");
    this.send({}, "verify_signal_94BDF");
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  See the prior example that combines     //
    //  multiple signals inside a single        //
    //  send to accommodate async/await.        //
    //  For example:                            //
    //  this.send({}, "await:SIG_1 SIG_2"); //
    //                                          //
    //  See also the next example which         //
    //  demonstrates using `.send() calls       //
    //  inside individual events to utilize     //
    //  async/await across methods.             //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
  }

  async test_signal_94BDF(payload, el) {
    await this.sleep(50);
    this.#_updated = payload.updated;
  }

  verify_signal_94BDF(payload, el) {
    const t2 = performance.now();
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  This result is less than the above      //
    //  sleep time because async/await has no   //
    //  practical effect on                     //
    //  this.send(PAYLOAD, SIGNALS)`        //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    if (this.#_updated === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Create an async/await chain of signal methods with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` [0072-async-await-chain]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC826F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_C826F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC826F = class {
  #_t1;

  bittyReady() {
    this.#_t1 = performance.now();
    this.send({ status: "ok" }, "run_signal_C826F");
  }

  async run_signal_C826F(payload, _) {
    if (payload.status === "ok") {
      await this.sleep(25);
      this.send(payload, "run_signal_C826F_2");
    }
  }

  async run_signal_C826F_2(payload, _) {
    if (payload.status === "ok") {
      await this.sleep(25);
      this.send(payload, "verify_signal_C826F");
    }
  }

  verify_signal_C826F(payload, el) {
    if (payload.status === "ok") {
      const t2 = performance.now();
      const time2 = t2 - this.#_t1;
      if (time2 > 40) {
        el.innerHTML = "ok";
      }
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
setLogLevel(level) [Done]
Description
Examples
Set the log level to any of: none, error, warn, info, debug, or trace. [0010-set-level]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class94188"></bitty-8-0>
<p>NOTE: Verification for this test is by confirming
  there is no log in the console for test_signal_94188
</p>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_94188">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class94188 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_94188");
  }

  given_signal_94188(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_94188");
  }

  test_signal_94188(_, el) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.addLog("error", "test_signal_94188", false);
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: Verification for this test is by confirming there is no log in the console for test_signal_94188

bug
Set to `warn` and throw error if invalid level is set. [0020-error-on-invalid]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE2600"></bitty-8-0>
<p>NOTE: Setting an invalid log level results in 
  the level defaulting to `warn` along with 
  a warning message about the incorrect setting attempt
  being output to the console. This test is run
  manually to avoid that message showing up in the
  console by default.
</p>
<button data-send="test_signal_E2600">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_E2600">waiting</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE2600 = class {
  test_signal_E2600(_, el) {
    this.setLogLevel("not-a-valid-level");
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: Setting an invalid log level results in the level defaulting to `warn` along with a warning message about the incorrect setting attempt being output to the console. This test is run manually to avoid that message showing up in the console by default.

waiting
sleep(ms) [Done]
Description
TEST STATUS: DONE
Examples
Use `this.sleep(ms)` to sleep an `async` method. [0010-sleep-for-time]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class43632"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_43632">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class43632 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_43632");
  }

  given_signal_43632(_, __) {
    this.t1 = performance.now();
    this.trigger("test_signal_43632");
  }

  async test_signal_43632(_, el) {
    await this.sleep(50);
    this.t2 = performance.now();
    if (this.t2 - this.t1 > 45) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
trigger(signals) [Done]
Description
  • Used to trigger one or more signals without sending an event or payload to the method.
Examples
Trigger a single signal with a single receiving element. [0010-trigger-a-single-signal]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC7BB1"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C7BB1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC7BB1 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("signal_C7BB1");
  }

  signal_C7BB1(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Trigger a single signal with multiple receivers. [0015-send-signal-to-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBD30E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BD30E">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BD30E">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BD30E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBD30E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("signal_BD30E");
  }

  signal_BD30E(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Trigger multiple signals with a single receiver. [0020-multiple-signals-single-reciever]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class86215"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_86215 signal_86215_2 signal_86215_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class86215 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger(`
      signal_86215 
      signal_86215_2
      signal_86215_3
    `);
  }

  signal_86215(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "alfa";
  }

  signal_86215_2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = `${el.innerHTML}-bravo`;
  }

  signal_86215_3(_, el) {
    if (el.innerHTML === `alfa-bravo`) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Trigger multiple signals with multiple receivers. [0025-multiple-signal-trigger]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB5F0B"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B5F0B">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B5F0B_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B5F0B_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB5F0B = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger(
      `signal_B5F0B signal_B5F0B_2 signal_B5F0B_3`,
    );
  }

  signal_B5F0B(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }

  signal_B5F0B_2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }

  signal_B5F0B_3(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Trigger a single signal that doesn't have any corresponding receivers (i.e. elements with `data-receive="SIGNAL"` attributes that match that match a signal in `this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)`). The signal method is triggered once. The second argument passed to it is `null`. [0030-send-to-method-without-a-receiver]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9C35B"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_9C35B">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9C35B = class {
  #gotSignal = false;
  #gotNull = false;

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("test_signal_9C35B");
  }

  test_signal_9C35B(_, x) {
    this.#gotSignal = true;
    if (x === null) {
      this.#gotNull = true;
    }
    this.trigger("verify_signal_9C35B");
  }

  verify_signal_9C35B(_, el) {
    if (this.#gotSignal === true && this.#gotNull === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Triggered signals from one bit are received in other bits. [0040-trigger-other-bits]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB1130"></bitty-8-0>
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB1130_2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B1130_2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB1130 = class {
  async bittyReady() {
    // delay to let other bit initialize
    await this.sleep(100);
    this.trigger("signal_B1130_2");
  }
};

window.ClassB1130_2 = class {
  signal_B1130_2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Triggering an async signal with await followed by additional signals in the same `this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)` call waits for the first method to finish before calling the following methods. [0070-await-async-signals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class8C32F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_8C32F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class8C32F = class {
  #t1;

  bittyReady() {
    this.#t1 = performance.now();
    this.trigger("await:test_signal_8C32F verify_signal_8C32F");
  }

  async test_signal_8C32F(_, el) {
    await this.sleep(50);
  }

  verify_signal_8C32F(_, el) {
    const t2 = performance.now();
    if ((t2 - this.#t1) > 40) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Calling `await` on a `.trigger()` has no practical effect. [0071-awaiting-triggers-does-nothing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7DC67"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: This example relies on performance time. It's 
  not automated since all the other tests on the 
  page can affect the timing. It can be 
  run manually. 
</p>
<button data-send="run_signal_7DC67">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_7DC67">waiting</div>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_7DC67_2">waiting</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7DC67 = class {
  #t1;

  async run_signal_7DC67() {
    this.#t1 = performance.now();
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  THIS DOES NOT WORK AS YOU MIGHT EXPECT  //
    //  Read the notes section for details.     //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    await this.trigger("await:signal_7DC67");
    this.trigger("signal_7DC67_2");
  }

  async signal_7DC67(_, el) {
    await this.sleep(1000);
    el.innerHTML = `updated at: ` + performance.now();
  }

  signal_7DC67_2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = `updated at: ` + performance.now();
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: This example relies on performance time. It's not automated since all the other tests on the page can affect the timing. It can be run manually.

waiting
waiting
Notes
  • Awaiting `.trigger()` (i.e. `await this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)`) has no practical effect. The `.trigger()` method creates and fires an event which is processed independently by a separate internal bitty method (and, each bit on the page processes the events independently). No attempt is made to communicate async/await status across method or bit boundaries.
  • Ideally a warning would be provided if you try to call `this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)` with `await`. The current approaches for determining if a method was called with `await` from inside the method itself are both unreliable and computationally expensive. Unfortunately, that makes delivering a warning infeasible.
Create a chain of async/await calls through individual signal methods. [0072-async-trigger-chain]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF6808"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_F6808">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF6808 = class {
  #t1;

  bittyReady() {
    this.#t1 = performance.now();
    this.trigger("run_signal_F6808");
  }

  async run_signal_F6808(_, __) {
    await this.sleep(25);
    this.trigger("run_signal_F6808_2");
  }

  async run_signal_F6808_2(_, __) {
    await this.sleep(25);
    this.trigger("verify_signal_F6808");
  }

  verify_signal_F6808(_, el) {
    const t2 = performance.now();
    const time2 = t2 - this.#t1;
    if (time2 > 40) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Triggers can fire signals that don't have a corresponding element with a `data-receive="SIGNAL"` attribute. When this happens the signal method is called once with `null` for the second argument. [0080-trigger-signal-without-receiver]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA1F39"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_A1F39">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA1F39 = class {
  #gotNull = false;

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("run_signal_A1F39");
  }

  run_signal_A1F39(_, x) {
    if (x === null) {
      this.#gotNull = true;
    }
    this.trigger("verify_signal_A1F39");
  }

  verify_signal_A1F39(_, el) {
    if (this.#gotNull === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
The first argument sent to a signal method form a `this.trigger(SIGNALS)` call is `null`. [0090-null-argument]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class88E0C"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_88E0C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class88E0C = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("signal_88E0C");
  }

  signal_88E0C(ev, el) {
    if (ev === null) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
updateElement(key, content) [Done]
Description
Examples
Update from string. [0010-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB814D"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_B814D">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB814D = class {
  #key = "element_signal_B814D";

  test_signal_B814D(_, el) {
    this.updateElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">bug</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_B814D");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and provide success result. [0020-ok-true-on-success]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassD4BFF"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_D4BFF">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassD4BFF = class {
  #key = "element_signal_D4BFF";

  test_signal_D4BFF(_, el) {
    const result = this.updateElement(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">bug</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_D4BFF");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Error on fail [0030-ok-false-on-failure]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBDBD6"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_BDBD6">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBDBD6 = class {
  #key = "element_signal_BDBD6";

  test_signal_BDBD6(_, el) {
    const result = this.updateElement(this.#key, {
      key: "not a string, element, or document fragment",
    });
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_BDBD6");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
From fragment. [0040-from-fragment]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class66521"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_66521">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class66521 = class {
  #key = "element_signal_66521";

  test_signal_66521(newElement, el) {
    this.updateElement(this.#key, newElement);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">bug</div>`);
    const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const element = document.createElement("div");
    element.classList.add("test");
    element.innerHTML = "ok";
    fragment.appendChild(element);
    this.send(fragment, "test_signal_66521");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
From element. [0050-from-element]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class45896"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_45896">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class45896 = class {
  #key = "element_signal_45896";

  test_signal_45896(newElement, el) {
    this.updateElement(this.#key, newElement);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">bug</div>`);
    const element = document.createElement("div");
    element.classList.add("test");
    element.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.send(element, "test_signal_45896");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Store automatically. [0060-store-automatically]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class6291C"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_6291C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class6291C = class {
  #key = "element_signal_6291C";

  test_signal_6291C(element, el) {
    this.loadElement(this.#key);
    el.replaceWith(this.renderElement(this.#key));
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">bug</div>`);
    this.updateElement(this.#key, `<div class="test">ok</div>`);
    delete this._element[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_6291C");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Since this is an update, don't warn if overwriting existing key. [0070-warn-on-overwrite]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class846F3"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_846F3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>

  #key = "element_signal_846F3";

  test_signal_846F3(_, el) {
    const result = this.updateElement(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_846F3");
  }
};
</script>
Results

No value returns error. [0080-no-value-returns-error]

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC95B6"></bitty-8-0>

  #key = "element_signal_C95B6";

  test_signal_C95B6(_, el) {
    const result = this.updateElement(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div></div>`);

HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE0312"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E0312">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE0312 = class {
  #key = "element_signal_E0312";

  test_signal_E0312(_, el) {
    const result = this.updateElement();
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {

    this.createElement(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
